task_completion.description: Open the Sygic GPS Navigation application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Sygic GPS Navigation application interface is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Access the main menu, settings, or vehicle profiles section.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main menu, settings panel, or 'Vehicle Profiles' screen (showing the initial 'Car' profile) is open.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the routing, navigation, or mode selection screen to find the pedestrian option.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The screen displays navigation mode options, including a pedestrian or walking person icon.
The 'Pedestrian' mode (walking person icon) is tapped, highlighted, or explicitly selected in the UI.

task_completion.description: Verify the change on the main map view.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The application has returned to the main map view, and there is a visual indicator (such as a walking person icon on the main screen or bottom bar) confirming that pedestrian mode is currently active.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Sygic GPS Navigation application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the genuine Sygic app interface, not a web search, text editor, or a different navigation app.

integrity.description: The agent used the graphical user interface to make changes.
integrity.visual_evidence: There is a logical sequence of UI screens (menus, settings, map) rather than a sudden jump to the final state via terminal commands or API calls.

integrity.description: The agent did not fake the existence of a 'Pedestrian' vehicle profile.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent selects the built-in pedestrian navigation mode rather than using text overlays or image manipulation to falsely create a 'Pedestrian' entry in the Vehicle Profiles list.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The initial vehicle profile is 'Car'. Note: 'Pedestrian' is NOT a valid vehicle profile type in Sygic GPS Navigation v25 (only Car, Caravan, and Motorcycle are valid). However, Sygic supports pedestrian navigation as a separate navigation mode feature. Do not penalize the agent for failing to find a 'Pedestrian' vehicle profile; full credit should be awarded if the agent successfully activates the pedestrian navigation mode/routing and verifies it on the main map.
